name: Apricot Mustard Glazed Leg of Lamb
recipeInstructions:
The crisp coating keeps the lamb succulent and juicy. Bake for an additional 10 minutes for medium-done. If using frozen lamb, defrost in the refrigerator overnight.
1. Combine apricot jam, honey mustard, garlic, soy sauce, olive oil, and dried rosemary. Reserve 2 tbsp of marinade for sauce. Brush the remainder evenly over the lamb. Season well with salt and pepper.
2. Marinate for 30 minutes.
3. Broil lamb for 3 minutes per side. Then bake at 425°F (220°C) fat side up for 20 minutes or until just pink. Remove from oven and let rest on a serving dish for 10 minutes. Pour off any excess fat in the pan.
4. Add red wine to the pan and reduce to 2 tbsp. Add beef broth, reserved marinade, and any extra lamb juices from the serving dish. Bring to a boil and simmer for 2 minutes.
5. Slice lamb thinly against the grain. Serve with some sauce poured over. Serve with a Merlot wine from Ontario, Canada, California, USA, or St Emilion. A Chianti is another fine match.
